title: Modular stock for a weapon
abstract: A stock includes a first end that has a stock mounting interface that is configured to mate with a like interface of a weapon. The first end has a quiver mounting feature configured to attach to a quiver. The stock includes a second end that has a quiver pocket defined therein to at least partially receive a portion of a quiver therein. The quiver pocket has at least one quiver grip positioned therein.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A stock for a weapon, the stock comprising: a body having a first end and a second end; the first end having a mounting interface and a quiver mounting feature, the mounting interface being at least one of a projection or a recess, the mounting interface being configured to mate with a like mounting interface using an interference fit; the second end having a quiver pocket configured to at least partially receive a portion of a quiver therein, the quiver pocket having at least one quiver grip positioned therein; and a quiver configured to store a plurality of projectiles, the quiver having a stock mounting feature being at least one of a projection or a recess, wherein the quiver is configured to be positioned within the quiver pocket at the second end and attached to the first end at the quiver mounting feature via the stock mounting feature. 2. A stock having a modular configuration. 3. A stock comprising a quiver mount. 4. The stock of claim 3, wherein the quiver mount is ambidextrous. 5. A stock comprising a crank mount. 6. The stock of claim 5, wherein the crank mount is ambidextrous. 7. A stock having a toolless interface for mounting the stock onto a projectile launcher. 8. A weapon system comprising: a weapon configured to propel a projectile from a front end, the weapon having a rear interface at a rear end, the rear interface having at least two stock mating elements; and a stock configured to be positioned against a shoulder of a user, the stock having a stock mounting interface configured to mate with the rear interface of the weapon, the stock mounting interface having at least two weapon mating elements, 9 

wherein at least one weapon mating element of the stock and at least one stock mating element of the weapon mate together using an interference fit to secure the stock to the weapon. 9. The weapon system of claim 8, wherein the at least two stock mating elements are projections. 10. The weapon system of claim 9, wherein at least one projection is a hook. 11. The weapon system of claim 9, wherein at least one projection is a post. 12. The weapon system of claim 8, wherein the at least two weapon mating elements are recesses. 13. The weapon system of claim 12, wherein at least one recess has a vertical pocket that mirrors that of at least one of the at least two stock mating elements of the weapon. 14. The weapon system of claim 12, wherein at least one recess is sized and shaped to receive at least one of the at least two stock mating elements of the weapon via an interference fit. 15. The weapon system of claim 8, further comprising a quiver configured to store a plurality of projectiles, the quiver having a stock mounting feature being at least one of a projection or a recess, wherein the quiver is configured to be positioned within a quiver pocket defined within the stock. 16. The weapon system of claim 8, wherein the stock further includes an arming tool mount positionable at a side. 17. The weapon system of claim 16, wherein the arming tool mount has a cradle configured to receive an arming tool therein. 18. The weapon system of claim 17, wherein the cradle is attachable to multiple locations on the stock. 19. The weapon system of claim 18, wherein the stock defines an aperture configured to receive a portion of the arming tool therein. 20. A stock for a weapon, the stock comprising: a body having a first end and a second end, the second end opposite the first end; the first end having a stock mounting interface configured to mate with a corresponding interface of a weapon, the first end having a quiver mounting feature configured to attach to a quiver; and the second end having a quiver pocket defined therein to at least partially receive a portion of a quiver therein, the quiver pocket having at least one quiver grip positioned therein. 21. The stock of claim 20, wherein the stock further includes a quiver removably mounted thereto, the quiver being configured to secure a plurality of projectiles therein, the quiver having a stock mounting feature being at least one of a projection or a recess, wherein the quiver is configured to be positioned within the quiver pocket at the second end and attached to the first end at the quiver mounting feature via the stock mounting feature. 22. The stock of claim 20, wherein the stock mounting interface has at least two weapon mating elements, wherein at least one weapon mating element of the stock and at least one stock mating element of a weapon mate together using an interference fit to secure the stock to the weapon. 23. A removable stock for a weapon, the stock comprising: a body having a stock interface configured to removably couple the stock to a rear interface of the weapon, the stock interface including at least a first and a second mating element, 11 

 wherein the first mating element is a pivotal connection element which, when connected with the rear interface of the weapon, permits the stock to pivot with respect to the weapon about the pivotal connection element, and wherein the second mating element is an interference fit element that releasably engages the rear interface of the weapon with an interference fit such that, when the second mating element is engaged with the rear interface of the weapon, the stock is prevented from pivoting about the pivotal connection element, and when the second mating element is disengaged from the rear interface of the weapon, the stock is free to pivot about the pivotal connection element. 24. The removable stock of claim 23, wherein the first mating element is a hook recess. 25. The removable stock of claim 23, wherein the second mating element is a post recess. 26. A removable stock for a weapon comprising a body including a stock interface for removably connecting the stock with a rear interface of the weapon, wherein the stock interface comprises one or more mating elements that provide a snap-fit connection with the rear interface of the weapon. 27. The removable stock of claim 26, wherein the snap-fit connection is a tool free connection that can be connected and disconnected by hand without tools. 28. A removable stock for a weapon, the stock comprising: a body having a stock interface configured to removably couple the stock to a rear interface of the weapon, the stock interface including at least a first and a second mating element, wherein the first mating element is connectable to the rear interface of the weapon, and wherein the second mating element is an interference fit element that releasably engages the rear interface of the weapon with an interference fit such that, when the second mating element is engaged with the rear interface of the weapon, the stock is prevented from moving at the first mating element, and when the second mating 12 

element is disengaged from the rear interface of the weapon, the stock can move at the first mating element. 29. The removable stock of claim 28, wherein the first mating element is a hook recess. 30. The removable stock of claim 28, wherein the second mating element is a post recess. 31. The removable stock of claim 28, wherein the first mating element is a pivotal connection element. 32. The removable stock of claim 28, wherein the first mating element comprises a hook. 33. A projectile launcher comprising: a rear interface configured to mate with a stock interface of a removable stock, the rear interface including at least a first and a second mating element, wherein the first mating element is a pivotal connection element which, when connected with the stock interface of the removable stock, permits the stock to pivot with respect to the projectile launcher about the pivotal connection element, and wherein the second mating element is an interference fit element that releasably engages the stock interface of the removable stock with an interference fit such that, when the second mating element is engaged with the stock interface of the removable stock, the stock is prevented from pivoting about the pivotal connection element, and when the second mating element is disengaged from the stock interface of the removable stock, the stock is free to pivot about the pivotal connection element. 34. The projectile launcher of claim 33, wherein the first mating element is a hook. 35. The projectile launcher of claim 33, wherein the second mating element is a post. 36. The projectile launcher of claim 33, further comprising a body, the body including the rear interface. 13 37. A rear interface of a projectile launcher, the rear interface being configured to mate with a stock interface of a removable stock, the rear interface comprising one or more mating elements that provide a snap-fit connection with the stock interface of the removable stock. 38. A projectile launcher comprising: a rear interface configured to mate with a stock interface of a removable stock, the rear interface including at least a first and a second mating element, wherein the first mating element is connectable to the stock interface of the removable stock, and wherein the second mating element is an interference fit element that releasably engages the stock interface of the removable stock with an interference fit such that, when the second mating element is engaged with the stock interface of the removable stock, the stock is prevented from moving at the first mating element, and when the second mating element is disengaged from the stock interface of the removable stock, the stock can move at the first mating element. 39. A weapon system comprising: a weapon configured to propel a projectile from a front end, the weapon having a rear interface at a rear end; and a stock configured to be positioned against a shoulder of a user, the stock having a stock mounting interface configured to mate with the rear interface of the weapon; and, at least four mating elements comprising at least two stock mating elements on the rear interface of the weapon and at least two weapon mating elements on the stock mounting interface of the stock; wherein at least one weapon mating element of the stock and at least one stock mating element of the weapon mate together using an interference fit to secure the stock to the weapon. 40. The weapon system of claim 39, wherein at least two mating elements are projections. 14 41. The weapon system of claim 40, wherein at least one projection is a hook. 42. The weapon system of claim 40, wherein at least one projection is a post. 43. The weapon system of claim 39, wherein at least two mating elements are recesses. 44. The weapon system of claim 43, wherein at least one recess has a vertical pocket that mirrors that of at least one other mating element. 45. The weapon system of claim 43, wherein at least one recess is sized and shaped to receive at least one of the other mating elements via an interference fit. 46. The stock of claim 1, wherein the stock further comprises at least one quiver mounting feature on a first and second side of the body of the stock, thereby allowing the quiver to be mounted on either the first of the second side. 47. The stock of claim 1, wherein the stock mounting interface is configured to mate onto a projectile launcher without the use of tools. 15
